skill.name: obsidian-clipper-template-creator
skill.description: Create importable Obsidian Web Clipper templates using verified page variables, selectors, filters, and Base properties.
skill.summary: Builds validated Obsidian Web Clipper templates from page data and vault Base schemas.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium issues, but review found they are false positives from Markdown examples and Obsidian template syntax. No executable code, shell command invocation, credential access, or prompt injection attempt was found. The remaining low risk comes from user-directed URL fetching and reading local Obsidian Base files to build templates.

low_findings.title: Template Syntax Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backtick and brace patterns are Markdown inline code and Obsidian Web Clipper template variables, not Ruby or shell execution. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed references show documentation examples such as variables, filters, YAML snippets, and JSON snippets with no executable script or command runner.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged text contains words and template variables like description, meta description, and schema fields, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic calls. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The cited files are JSON examples and Markdown documentation, with no cryptographic library imports or hashing operations.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Network Fetching
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW_RISK. The skill instructs the agent to fetch a representative URL from the user or inspect a browser DOM snapshot to verify selectors. This is expected for the feature, but it can access external web pages chosen by the user. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: The workflow explicitly requires WebFetch or DOM retrieval, but there is no evidence of hidden endpoints, credential transmission, or unauthorized exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Local Obsidian Base File Reads
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW_RISK. The skill asks the agent to read local Templates/Bases files so template properties can match the user vault schema. This is legitimate productivity behavior, but users should know local vault metadata may be inspected. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The cited workflow directly instructs reading specific Base files, but the access is scoped to user-selected Obsidian configuration and no network transfer is requested.

content.value_statement: Obsidian users often need clipper templates that match page data and vault properties. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through verified template creation.
actual_capabilities: Creates importable Obsidian Web Clipper template structures.
Maps Obsidian Base properties to clipper template properties.
Uses preset, selector, meta, and Schema.org variables.
Applies documented Web Clipper filters for formatting.
Requires selector verification against fetched page content.
Provides example templates for general clips and recipes.
limitations: Requires a representative URL for reliable selector choices.
Cannot verify private pages without accessible page content.
Does not install or configure the Obsidian Web Clipper extension.
Prompt variables require the Obsidian AI Interpreter to be enabled.

use_cases.title: Recipe Vault Templates
use_cases.description: Create a recipe clipper template that captures ingredients, instructions, source, cuisine, and nutrition fields.

use_cases.title: Research Article Capture
use_cases.description: Build article templates that save title, author, URL, publication date, summary, and selected page content.

use_cases.title: Vault Schema Alignment
use_cases.description: Match Web Clipper output to existing Base properties so new notes fit current vault workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Clipping Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian Web Clipper template for general articles. Use title, author, URL, publication date, and main content.

prompt_templates.title: Build From a Sample URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Verify variables from a real page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Web Clipper template for this URL. Fetch the page, verify available metadata, and only use selectors that exist.

prompt_templates.title: Match My Base Properties
prompt_templates.scenario: Align template output with vault metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Read my Templates/Bases files and create a clipper template that maps available page data to the matching Base properties.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Specialized Schema Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a specialized Web Clipper template for pages with Schema.org data. Include triggers, formatted note content, and typed properties.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a general article clipping template.
output_examples.output: A complete import-ready template description with title, source, author, published date, created date, and main content fields.

output_examples.input: A user provides a recipe page URL.
output_examples.output: A recipe-focused template plan that uses verified Schema.org fields for name, ingredients, instructions, image, cuisine, and nutrition.

output_examples.input: A user wants templates to match existing Obsidian Bases.
output_examples.output: A property mapping that connects Base fields to clipper variables and identifies fields that need manual entry.

best_practices: Provide a representative URL before asking for selectors.
Review generated paths and property names before importing the template.
Prefer Schema.org and meta variables before fragile CSS selectors.
anti_patterns: Do not guess selectors when page content was not inspected.
Do not use prompt variables when the AI Interpreter is disabled.
Do not hardcode private vault paths into templates shared with others.

faq.question: Does this skill create templates I can import into Obsidian?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the assistant to produce Web Clipper template content that follows the documented import structure.

faq.question: Why does the skill ask for a sample URL?
faq.answer: A sample URL lets the assistant verify metadata, Schema.org data, and selectors before creating the template.

faq.question: Can it work with my existing Obsidian Bases?
faq.answer: Yes. It can read Base files you provide or allow access to, then map matching fields into clipper properties.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No evidence of command execution was found. The files contain documentation examples and template syntax.

faq.question: Can it create templates for private websites?
faq.answer: It can help when you provide accessible page content, a DOM snapshot, or verified selectors from the private page.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
